summary: US market rally led by AI optimism; Nvidia and Intel jumped as Nasdaq outperformed after tech-led buying and AI-driven demand expectations. Short-horizon upside for technology and ad/communication platforms from valuation rerating and flows.

reasons: First-order: Headlines of an 'AI boom' lift expectations for stronger demand for AI accelerators and higher semiconductor capex, directly boosting sentiment toward chip makers (NVIDIA, Intel). That drives buying into technology-heavy indices (Nasdaq) and triggers valuation multiple expansion. Second-order: higher AI adoption expectations support ad and cloud platforms via increased monetization and cloud spend, helping communication services. Short-horizon transmission is dominated by flows (ETF reallocation, momentum buying) and analyst/market re‑rating. The article specifically cites Nvidia and Intel — hence those symbols are direct beneficiaries; broader tech and communication services gain from positive sentiment and rotation. No clear company-specific negative shock identified for short horizon, so hurt lists are left empty.

affected_inputs: advertising and cloud AI spending expectations
